analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet highlights Lushan National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, emphasizing its cultural integration with nature and its inspiration for Chinese aesthetics. Evaluation: CH1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not present; no mention of the West or hostility. CH2 (China as benevolent power) – Not present; no references to international cooperation or global governance. CH3 (China has an epic history) – Not directly; while it touches on cultural heritage and aesthetic tradition, it does not discuss historical resilience or adversities. CH4 (appropriate political system/values) – Not present; no political system or governance content. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – Not present; no mention of governance achievements or development under the CCP. CH6 (China’s culture, nature, heritage) – Yes; the tweet promotes Chinese cultural and natural heritage via a UNESCO site and aesthetic traditions. Conclusion: Only CH6 applies.
